title: Tuneros de San Luis Potosí

infobox.value: [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD_(city) "San Luis Potosí (city)"), [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD "San Luis Potosí")
[Estadio 20 de Noviembre](/w/index.php?title=Estadio_20_de_Noviembre_(San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Estadio 20 de Noviembre (San Luis Potosí) (page does not exist)")

(English: San Luis Potosí Prickly Pear Pickers) were a [professional baseball](/wiki/Professional_baseball "Professional baseball") team in the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League") based in [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD_(city) "San Luis Potosí (city)"), Mexico. The club played during 16 seasons spanning 1946–2006.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Baseball team in San Luis Potosí, San Luis Potosí

| Tuneros de San Luis Potosí | |

| [Ballpark](/wiki/Ballpark "Ballpark") | [Estadio 20 de Noviembre](/w/index.php?title=Estadio_20_de_Noviembre_(San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Estadio 20 de Noviembre (San Luis Potosí) (page does not exist)") |

The **Tuneros de San Luis Potosí** (English: San Luis Potosí Prickly Pear Pickers) were a [professional baseball](/wiki/Professional_baseball "Professional baseball") team in the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League") based in [San Luis Potosí](/wiki/San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD_(city) "San Luis Potosí (city)"), Mexico. The club played during 16 seasons spanning 1946–2006.

The first Tuneros team played from 1946 through 1952 in the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League"). After 13 years of absence, another club with a similar name played in the [Mexican Central League](/wiki/Mexican_Central_League "Mexican Central League") from 1960–1962 and once more in 1971. Then, they joined the expanded Mexican League from 1986–1990; played as the Reales de San Luis Potosí in 1991, and again as the Tuneros from 2004–2006.

The Tuneros enjoyed their only winning season in 1971, when they won the MCL Central Division title with a 45–27 record but lost the final Series. After that, no team from San Luis Potosí has ever won a title in Mexican baseball.

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tuneros_de_San_Luis_Potos%C3%AD&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notes")]

* In [Spanish language](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"), the term *tunero* (too-neh'-ro) refers to a [prickly pear](/wiki/Opuntia "Opuntia") [picker](/wiki/Farmworker "Farmworker").
* The legendary slugger Héctor Espino, considered by many as the greatest player in Mexican baseball history, started his professional career with the Tuneros in the 1960 Mexican Central League season.
